etymology.description: semantic 辛(辛) (xīn, "knife; chisel") + phonetic 方(方) (fāng, giving 'páng' sound) -> moving to corner or side
etymology.interpretation: The character 旁(páng, "side") combines 辛(辛) (xīn, "knife; chisel") with 方(方), where 方 provides the phonetic part. The component 辛 historically suggested the concept of being cut off or separate, leading to the idea of being on the 'side' or 'apart'. The sound of the character is derived from 方, linking it phonetically.

examples.chinese: 狗在桌子旁(狗在桌子旁)。
examples.english: The dog is beside the table.

examples.chinese: 她坐在我旁边(她坐在我旁邊)。
我们在公园的湖旁转了一圈(我們在公園的湖旁轉了一圈)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zài gōngyuán de hú páng zhuànle yī quān.
examples.english: We took a walk around the lake in the park.
